name: finance-semiconductor-china
description: Use web_search to search for 'China semiconductor self-sufficiency SMIC Huawei chip development progress'. Results will overlap with earlier semiconductor searches. How far has China progressed in semiconductor self-sufficiency? What are SMIC and Huawei's latest achievements? Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: China has made notable progress in semiconductor self-sufficiency despite U.S. export controls. SMIC achieved a breakthrough with three generations of 7nm-class nodes using DUV lithography (without EUV access), and is progressing toward 5nm. Huawei's Kirin 9030 chip, fabricated on SMIC's N+3 process for the Mate 80 series, represents China's most advanced domestic semiconductor to date, though it still lags behind TSMC and Samsung's leading-edge 3nm/2nm processes. Huawei also offers competitive AI accelerators (Ascend series) challenging Nvidia in certain segments. China's self-sufficiency is expected to reach ~50% by 2025, with additional foundries like Hua Hong pursuing 7nm capability.
key_facts: SMIC developed three generations of 7nm-class nodes using DUV lithography without EUV access
Huawei's Kirin 9030 on SMIC's N+3 is China's most advanced domestic chip, but lags global leaders
China's semiconductor self-sufficiency expected to reach approximately 50% by 2025
Huawei's Ascend AI accelerators compete with some Nvidia data center GPUs
China reportedly built a prototype EUV machine, expected to produce chips between 2028-2030
